subheadings.heading: Safe PoC Reference Table
subheadings.content:
| Issue Type | Recommended Proof of Concept (PoC) |
|---|---|
| **Cross-site Scripting** | `alert(document.domain)` or `` setInterval`alert\x28document.domain\x29` `` |
| **Command Execution** | `id`, `cat /proc/1/maps`, or `touch /root/username` (per rules) |
| **SQL Injection** | `SELECT @@version` (MySQL/MSSQL), `SELECT version()` (Postgres) |
| **SSRF** | Read local files, cloud metadata (IMDS), or internal Redis/DB access |
| **Unvalidated Redirect** | Redirect to `google.com` or safe login clones to demonstrate risk |
| **XXE Injection** | Output random harmless data or local `/etc/hostname` |
| **Subdomain Takeover** | Claim discreetly and serve a harmless file on a hidden path |

subheadings.heading: PoC Best Practices
content: **Information Exposure**: Only leverage bugs against your own test accounts; never target other users' data.
**Local File Read**: Retrieve only harmless system files (e.g., `/etc/hostname`) or files designated by the policy.
**CSRF Design**: Hide forms using `style="display:none;"` and implement auto-submit for professional impact demonstration.

subheadings.heading: The Golden Report Template
content: Brief overview of the vulnerability and its location.
Technical deep-dive into the root cause.
What can an attacker achieve? (P1-P5 scaling).
Verified on Chrome vXX, Firefox vYY, etc.
Recommended fix (e.g., input sanitization, CSP updates).

subheadings.heading: Reporting Workflow
subheadings.caption: Professional workflow from vulnerability discovery to triage and remediation
